ops.title: Locked-screen -25308 is FIXED: daemon survives (v0.7.0) and doctor reports keybag-locked as OK-with-note (v0.8.3+); a locked machine showing the OK note lines is healthy
ops.body:
The old failure (watch daemon crash-looping with `SecKeyCreateRandomKey` OSStatus -25308 when the screen was locked at startup) is **fixed, in two layers**:

1. **v0.7.0 (`4f79099`, 2026-07-02)** — the keyhelper distinguishes -25308/`errSecInteractionNotAllowed` (exit 3) from genuine no-SE (exit 2); `daemon.Build` degrades to a RAM-only healing key cache instead of dying, and re-primes Secure-Enclave-wrapped on the first `Put` after the keybag unlocks. No more crash-loop; regression test `internal/daemon/daemon_test.go` `TestBuildDegradedPresenceStartsWithMemoryCache`.
2. **v0.8.3 (`2752e316`, 2026-07-03, first shipped broadly in v0.9.0)** — `cookiesync doctor` no longer renders a locked machine as FAIL. `auth_status` gained `"keybag_locked"` (computed by `keybagLocked` in `internal/daemon/session.go`: console session locked, absent, or another user's via fast user switching — screen-share deliberately excluded), the daemon swallows a live `cache-unwrap` -25308 refusal only when keybag-locked, and doctor renders:
   - `OK key cache: Secure-Enclave wrapped (keybag locked: screen locked or session away)` — healthy daemon, keybag currently locked
   - `OK key cache: in process memory (keybag locked at daemon start; re-primes Secure-Enclave wrapped on the first prime after it unlocks)` — daemon restarted while locked
   - `FAIL key cache: degraded: … run 'cookiesync auth' to re-prime` — the one genuine failure (degraded while the keybag is AVAILABLE)

**How to apply:** a locked/away Mac showing either OK-note line is healthy — do not report it as a failure or "benign known bug"; it is designed behavior. Live-verified on locked yasyf-home (doctor 7/7, exit 0). A key-cache FAIL now only means degraded-while-available or a real RPC error — investigate those. The pre-fix advice (check `ioreg` for `CGSSessionScreenIsLocked`, expect launchd crash-loops) is obsolete for cookiesync ≥0.8.3; still never `security list-keychains -s` ([[cookiesync-se-signing]]). Related: [[cookiesync-architecture]], [[cookiesync-go-rewrite-progress]].
